nodes.title: Dev through Relationships
nodes.description: Introduces Bowlby's attachment theory as a framework for understanding human development. Through readings, lectures, and critical reflection activities, explores the cross-cultural differences as well as the transcultural and foundational nature of early relationships, their intergenerational transmission, and their impact on later relationships and development (including developmental trauma). Prerequisite: HDF 1050 or HDF 1600.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum sophomore standing; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: CL:Fndn HumDev&FamSci for Mjrs
nodes.description: Seminar designed to introduce incoming majors to college expectations and skills, and to concepts and practices of Human Development & Family Science and critically thinking about these concepts and practices. Prerequisite: Human Development and Family Science major. Pre/Co-requisites: HDF 1050.
nodes.other_requirements: Human Development and Family Science major. Required class for HDFS majors; HDFS majors only

nodes.title: Exploring Dvlpmnt in Community
nodes.description: Experiential learning course focused on self-exploration work with instruction and reflection to enrich the learning experience and strengthen community. Students will be asked to engage in community so as to explore the role that community serves in support of self and others. Prerequisite: Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. Catamount Recovery Program; Instructor invitation /permission required; Contact instructor for location

nodes.title: Family Context of Development
nodes.description: Developmental ecological approach to analysis of the family as a system in which individuals develop.
nodes.other_requirements: Degree students only; PACE students register for HDF 1600 ZRA; HDF/ECP students register for HDF 1600 B; Colocated with HDF 1600 B and ZRA

nodes.title: Power & Interpersonal Violence
nodes.description: Examines the complexities of power, conflict, and violence in close relationships, families, and other interpersonal contexts throughout the lifespan by analyzing scholarly and popular knowledge about power and violence in interpersonal relationships. Prerequisites: HDF 2009, HDF 2610, HDF 2890, minimum Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum Junior standing

nodes.title: Families and Health
nodes.description: Lectures, laboratories, readings, or projects relating to contemporary areas of study. Enrollment may be more than once, accumulation up to twelve hours.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum Junior standing; Please note that program overrides will be needed.

nodes.title: Intro to Research Methods
nodes.description: Seminars and research projects. Methods of historical, descriptive, experimental, quasi-experimental, field studies, and survey research.

nodes.title: Psych Research Methods
nodes.description: Principles of research methodology, including design and reporting. Prepares students to understand and evaluate psychological research in a variety of areas of psychology. Credit not awarded for both PSYS 2002 and PSYS 2000. Prerequisite: PSYS 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: NEUR majors and minors only

nodes.title: Social Context of Development
nodes.description: Developmental ecological approach to analysis of social institutions as influences on human development. Focus on education, community, health care, and social services. Prerequisites: HDF 1600; Minimum Sophomore standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Sophomore standing; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Human Relationships &Sexuality
nodes.description: Sexual responsibility and the biological, social, psychological growth, and development of human beings in terms of sex role identity.

nodes.title: Theories of Human Development
nodes.description: Introduction to the most influential theories of human development where students study, compare, and evaluate select theories and apply them to issues of practical importance. Prerequisites: HDF 1050, HDF 1600; minimum Sophomore standing.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum Sophomore standing. HDF 1600 may be taken as a co-requisite (i.e., while taking HDF 2890) by permission.

nodes.title: Interrogating White Identity
nodes.description: Introductory examination of white identity development and white identity development models from an ecological perspective. Prerequisites: HDF 1050 or HDF 1600; Minimum Sophomore standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Sophomore standing.

nodes.title: Social Research Methods
nodes.description: Introduction to research methods in social science. Includes examination of research design, measurement, data collection, data analysis, and the presentation and theoretical interpretation of research findings. Prerequisites: STAT 1050, STAT 1110, STAT 1410, or higher; three hours of Sociology or Political Science; minimum Sophomore standing. Cross-listed with: POLS 2800.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Sophomore standing; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Sem In Close Relationships
nodes.description: Causal conditions influencing formation, maintenance, and dissolution of intimate adult relationships. Draws on theory and students' personal experiences to explicate the nature of close relationships in contemporary American society. Prerequisites: HDF 2009, HDF 2610, HDF 2890; minimum Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum Junior standing

nodes.title: Internship
nodes.description: On-site supervised work experience combined with a structured academic learning plan directed by a faculty member or a faculty-staff team in which a faculty member is the instructor of record, for which academic credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisites: Minimum Sophomore standing; HDF 1050, HDF 1600, Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Sophomore standing; Instructor permission. Minimum sophomore standing, and Instructor permission.

nodes.title: The Helping Relationship
nodes.description: Prepares students for the Human Services Profession through the study and practice of professional standards and select helping skills central to effective helping relationships. Prerequisites: HDF 1050 or HDF 1600; Minimum Sophomore standing. Cross-listed with: CNSL 2010.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Sophomore standing.

nodes.title: Intro Social Work Research
nodes.description: Introduction to models and methods of social research from a social work perspective. Prerequisite: SWSS 1020.
nodes.other_requirements: Permission and prereq override; SWSS majors only

nodes.title: SL:Internship
nodes.description: On-site supervised work experience combined with a structured academic learning plan directed by a faculty member or a faculty-staff team in which a faculty member is the instructor of record, for which academic credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisites: HDF 1650, HDF 2009, HDF 2205, HDF 2410, HDF 2610, HDF 2890; HDF 2010 or CNSL 2010; Senior standing; Instructor permission based on consultation with program faculty.
nodes.other_requirements: Senior standing; Instructor permission based on consultation with program faculty. By program permission, contact Jackie Weinstock.

nodes.title: Undergraduate Research
nodes.description: Undergraduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisites: Minimum Sophomore standing; minimum of 6 credits in HDF or related field; Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Sophomore standing; minimum of 6 credits in HDF or related field; Instructor permission. Instructor permission required. This offering is restricted to students conducting research with Dr. Conroy as their thesis advisor and/or research mentor. Expectations are determined in consultation with each student and must be discussed prior to enrollment.

nodes.title: Undergraduate Research
nodes.description: Undergraduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisites: Minimum Junior standing; HDF 2610, HDF 2890, Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Junior standing; Instructor permission; Instructor permission required.

nodes.title: Social Research Methods
nodes.description: Introduction to research methods in social science. Includes examination of research design, measurement, data collection, data analysis, and the presentation and theoretical interpretation of research findings. Prerequisites: STAT 1050, STAT 1110, STAT 1410, or higher; three hours of Sociology or Political Science; minimum Sophomore standing. Cross-listed with: SOC 2500.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Sophomore standing; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Psychology of Aging
nodes.description: This course provides students with a comprehensive overview of psychological aspects of aging and identifies key lessons and facilitative practices for supporting positive aging. Prerequisites: HDF 2009, HDF 2610, HDF 2890, minimum Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum Junior standing

nodes.title: The Helping Relationship
nodes.description: Prepares students for the Human Services Profession through the study and practice of professional standards and select helping skills central to effective helping relationships. Prerequisites: HDF 1050 or HDF 1600; Minimum Sophomore standing or Instructor Permission. Cross-listed with: HDF 2010.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Sophomore standing or Instructor Permission.

nodes.title: Human Development
nodes.description: A comprehensive survey of life span individual and family development within social and historical context.
